commands.review_summary:
The branch changes Telegram partial-stream final delivery to wait for successful continuation chunks, repairs truncated preview-finalized text from the transcript, adds regression tests, and suppresses active abort recovery for queued embedded diagnostic sessions.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main marks a Telegram streamed final delivered before sending continuation chunks, and the PR tests model both thrown and false-return follow-up failures. I did not run tests because this review is read-only and the missing piece is real Telegram proof.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is needed because this external Telegram transport PR lacks real behavior proof and includes an availability-sensitive diagnostics policy change rather than a remaining narrow code defect for automation.

target.title: fix(telegram): avoid silent truncation in partial streaming finals
target.body:
Fixes an intermittent Telegram partial-streaming failure mode where a streamed final could be marked as delivered before any follow-up chunks are actually sent.

When a follow-up send fails, the channel code can otherwise suppress the visible error fallback (because deliveryState already says delivered), leaving the user with a mid-sentence cutoff.

Changes:
- Telegram: only mark streamed finals delivered after follow-up chunks are sent successfully; add regression tests.
- Telegram: when a preview-finalized final looks truncated, try to repair it by reading the transcript-backed canonical final and editing the Telegram message to the longer text.
- Diagnostics: avoid aborting queued embedded work when the session is still processing queued items (reduces false-positive stuck aborts).

Related: #49889 (Telegram partial-stream finalization delivery-state ambiguity / message-loss incidents).
target.author: vice-magus-faolan
